perturbation.original_text: The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information which is: (a) rightfully in public domain other than by a breach of duty to the disclosing party (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party (d) independently developed by employees of the receiving party; or (e) generally made available to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information, regardless of the circumstances or how it became known to them.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes all the exceptions to confidentiality in Section 4 and replaces them with a blanket statement that the receiving party is never liable for disclosure. This directly contradicts the obligation of confidentiality outlined in Section 2, creating an In-Text contradiction regarding when the receiving party is liable for breaches of confidentiality. Specifically, the change contradicts the receiving party’s responsibility to maintain Confidential Information under Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations of Receiving Party. The receiving party will maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information of the disclosing party with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The receiving party may disclose the disclosing party's Confidential Information to any employees or to any third parties, regardless of whether they have a need to know or agree to abide by nondisclosure terms. provided that the receiving party will be liable for breach by any such entity. The receiving party will not make any copies of the Confidential Information received from the disclosing party except as necessary for its employees, parent company and majority-owned subsidiaries with a need to know. Any copies which are made will be identified as belonging to the disclosing party and marked "confidential", "proprietary", or with a similar legend.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the receiving party is never liable for disclosure of Confidential Information, regardless of circumstances. This directly contradicts the original text, which holds the receiving party liable for breaches by its employees or third parties to whom it discloses the information, as stated in the 'Contradicted Text'. The Change Text therefore introduces a strong in-text contradiction to the contradicted text, making the obligations of the receiving party unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party: provided that each party's obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will survive any such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party: provided that upon termination, all obligations with respect to Confidential Information are immediately null and void.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies Section 6, stating that all confidentiality obligations end upon termination. This directly contradicts the original provision that confidentiality obligations survive termination, thus creating an In-Text contradiction of continued confidentiality when the agreement is terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Obligation of Disclosure: Termination. Neither party has any obligation to disclose Confidential Information to the other. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party: provided that upon termination, all obligations with respect to Confidential Information are immediately null and void. Either party may, at any time: (a) cease giving Confidential Information to the other party without any liability and/or (b) request in writing the return or destruction of all or part of its Confidential Information previously disclosed, and all copies thereof, and the receiving party will promptly comply with such request, and certify in writing its compliance.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive termination, while the changed text specifies that all such obligations are immediately null and void upon termination. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the duration of confidentiality, as both sections deal directly with confidentiality after termination. The contradicted text also contains a clause about all obligations with respect to Confidential Information being immediately null and void, which is the same as the Changed Text. So the change causes the original statement in Section 6 to contradict with the statement in the contradicted text (which is also Section 6). This fits the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, either party may use Residuals for any purpose, including without limitation use in development manufacture, promotion, sale and maintenance of its products or services: provided that this right to Residuals does not represent a licence under any patents, copyrights or other intellectual property rights of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, neither party may use Residuals for any purpose, including without limitation use in development manufacture, promotion, sale and maintenance of its products or services: provided that this right to Residuals does not represent a licence under any patents, copyrights or other intellectual property rights of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 7 changes “either party may use” to “neither party may use”. The main contradiction occurs with the rest of the section, which describes how “Residuals” are extracted/handled. By stating residuals cannot be used, contradicts describing residuals.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Residuals. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, neither party may use Residuals for any purpose, including without limitation use in development manufacture, promotion, sale and maintenance of its products or services: provided that this right to Residuals does not represent a licence under any patents, copyrights or other intellectual property rights of the disclosing party. The term "Residuals" means any information retained in the unaided memories of the receiving party's employees who have had access to the disclosing party's Confidential Information pursuant to the terms of this Agreement. An employee's memory is unaided if the employee has not intentionally memorized the Confidential Information for the purpose of retaining and subsequently using or disclosing it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 7 from allowing either party to use Residuals to stating neither party can. The contradicted text is the same changed text. This creates a direct contradiction because if neither party can use residuals, it contradicts the rest of the section, which implies residuals exist and are handled in a certain way. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement, any accompanying CITR and CITRs executed from time to time hereafter which incorporate the terms of this Agreement, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the disclosure(s) of Confidential Information described in each CITR, and may not be amended except in a writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective parties. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement, any accompanying CITR and CITRs executed from time to time hereafter which incorporate the terms of this Agreement, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the disclosure(s) of Confidential Information described in each CITR, and may be amended orally by either party. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 8(e) originally specifies that the agreement may not be amended except in writing. By contradicting this and stating the agreement may be amended orally introduces ambiguity and potential conflicts in contract enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. <PAGE> 3 (a) This Agreement is neither intended to nor will it be considered as creating a joint venture, partnership or other form of business association between the parties, nor an obligation to buy or sell products using or incorporating the Confidential Information. (b) Both parties understand and acknowledge that no license under any patent, copyright, trade secret, or other intellectual property right is granted to or conferred upon, either party in this Agreement or by the disclosure of any Confidential Information by one party to the other party as contemplated hereunder, either expressly, by implication, inducement, estoppel or otherwise, and that any license under such intellectual property rights must be express and in writing. (c) The failure of either party to enforce any right resulting from breach of any provision of this Agreement by the other party will not be deemed a waiver of any right relating to a subsequent breach of such provision or of any other right hereunder. (d) This Agreement will be governed by laws of the State of Delaware without reference to conflict of laws principles. (e) This Agreement, any accompanying CITR and CITRs executed from time to time hereafter which incorporate the terms of this Agreement, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the disclosure(s) of Confidential Information described in each CITR, and may be amended orally by either party. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be amended in writing. The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the agreement may be amended orally. This directly contradicts the original requirement for written amendments, creating ambiguity and a potential conflict in contract enforcement because the contradicted text is the same as the changed text meaning the text now contradicts itself.
